sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: West Ham United have struggled recently, managing only 1 win in their last 5 matches with a record of 1-2-2, showing inconsistency and defensive vulnerabilities with no clean sheets in 20 games. Sunderland, on the other hand, are in better shape with a 1-3-1 record in their last 5, demonstrating resilience with draws and a recent win, though they’ve failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 4 away games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, Sunderland have had the upper hand, winning 3-0 in their most recent clash in August 2025, while the earlier match in 2017 ended in a 2-2 draw. Sunderland’s recent dominance suggests they may pose a significant threat to West Ham at the London Stadium.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: West Ham, sitting 18th in the league with 17 points, have struggled defensively, conceding 44 goals (worst in the league) while scoring 24. Sunderland, positioned 9th with 33 points, have a balanced record with 23 goals scored and conceded, indicating a more stable setup compared to their opponents.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: West Ham will miss key midfielder Lucas Paquetá, which could impact their creativity, though Jarrod Bowen remains a threat with 6 goals this season. Sunderland’s Granit Xhaka, with 5 assists, and Daniel Ballard, a defensive rock, could be pivotal, despite the absence of forward Bertrand Traoré.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Considering Sunderland’s superior form, league standing, and recent head-to-head advantage, they are favored to win as per Sofascore’s prediction (49% chance). West Ham’s defensive frailties and lack of consistency make it challenging for them to secure a victory, though a draw (30% chance) is plausible given their home advantage.
